title: Task 17: Render links in text renderer
description: Add Links section in text endpoint detail with name, target operationId, parameter mappings, description, and drill command. Plan: Task 17 (lines 1568-1642).
close_reason: Links section rendered in text endpoint detail with name, operationId, parameters, drill command; 1 test added (133 total)

title: Fix suggest_similar to return best matches, not first matches
description:
## Problem
In schemas.rs lines 63-68, suggest_similar_schemas uses .take(3) before any sorting:
```rust
.filter(|k| strsim::jaro_winkler(&lower, &k.to_lowercase()) > 0.8)
.take(3)
.cloned()
.collect()
```

This returns the first 3 schemas that pass the 0.8 threshold (in map insertion order), not the best 3 matches. For large specs (1000+ schemas), better matches later in the map are missed.

## Fix
Collect all matches, sort by score descending, then take 3:
```rust
let mut matches: Vec<_> = names.iter()
    .filter_map(|k| {
        let score = strsim::jaro_winkler(&lower, &k.to_lowercase());
        if score > 0.8 { Some((k.clone(), score)) } else { None }
    })
    .collect();
matches.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.partial_cmp(&a.1).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));
matches.into_iter().take(3).map(|(k, _)| k).collect()
```

Check if the same pattern exists in other suggest_similar functions (resources, search, callbacks).

## Success Criteria
- Suggestions are ranked by similarity score (best first)
- All existing tests pass
- Add a test with a large mock schema list to verify ordering
close_reason: Closed

title: Add Cookie variant to ParameterLocation enum
description:
## Problem
The ParameterLocation enum in models/resource.rs only has Path, Query, Header variants:
```rust
pub enum ParameterLocation {
    Path,
    Query,
    Header,
}
```

OpenAPI supports cookie parameters, and search.rs (line 119) handles openapiv3::Parameter::Cookie. But the model enum has no Cookie variant, so cookie parameters are silently dropped from resource output.

## Fix
Add Cookie variant to the enum:
```rust
pub enum ParameterLocation {
    Path,
    Query,
    Header,
    Cookie,
}
```

Then update any match arms in resources.rs that construct ParameterLocation from openapiv3::Parameter to include the Cookie case.

## Success Criteria
- Cookie parameters appear in resource detail output
- Add a test endpoint with cookie params to kitchen-sink.yaml fixture
- Existing tests pass
close_reason: Closed

title: External $ref dereferencing for multi-file OpenAPI documents
description: Feedback from OpenAPI community reviewer: Most real-world OADs at scale are split across multiple files using $ref. Without bundling/dereferencing external refs, phyll can't handle these. Currently only local (same-file) $ref is supported. Need to find a Rust library that can dereference/bundle multi-file OADs, or implement resolution. This is likely the highest-impact functional gap.
close_reason: External $ref dereferencing implemented. bundle_refs() in spec.rs transparently resolves relative file path refs and JSON Pointer fragments before parsing. 21 new tests (284 total), all CI gates pass.

title: Fix unsafe set_var in spec.rs tests for thread safety
description:
## Problem
5 test functions in spec.rs (lines 538-630) use `unsafe { std::env::set_var(...) }` and `unsafe { std::env::remove_var(...) }`. set_var is not thread-safe — if cargo test runs these in parallel with other tests that read env vars, results are undefined.

## Fix
Option A: Add `#[serial_test::serial]` attribute to each env-var test:
```toml
# Cargo.toml [dev-dependencies]
serial_test = "3"
```
```rust
use serial_test::serial;

#[test]
#[serial]
fn test_env_var_spec_resolution() { ... }
```

Option B: Move these 5 tests to integration tests (tests/integration_tests.rs) where they run as separate processes and can safely set env vars without unsafe.

Option A is simpler. Option B is architecturally cleaner but requires more restructuring.

## Locations
- `src/spec.rs:536-541` — test_env_var_spec_resolution
- `src/spec.rs:557-566` — test_env_var_overrides_config_file
- `src/spec.rs:588-597` — test_env_var_overrides_cli_flag
- `src/spec.rs:601-608` — test_env_var_nonexistent_path_errors
- `src/spec.rs:625-632` — test_env_var_empty_string_ignored

## Success Criteria
- No more unsafe blocks in test code
- Tests still pass: `cargo test --locked`
- No flaky test failures from parallel env var mutation
close_reason: Closed

title: --expand preserves Related schemas for unexpanded refs
description:
render_schema_detail() in text.rs:754 has if !expanded { ... } which skips the Related schemas section entirely when --expand is true. But not all refs get expanded (depth limit, edge cases). Users lose navigation hints for unexpanded types.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- phyll --schemas customer --expand still shows Related schemas for types that weren't inlined
- phyll --schemas Pet --expand where all refs ARE expanded shows no Related schemas section
- Text and JSON modes both include remaining ref hints

IMPLEMENTATION:
- File: src/render/text.rs, render_schema_detail() (~line 754)
- Change: Instead of if !expanded, collect nested_schema_names from fields that still have them after expansion. Show Related schemas section for those remaining refs.
- Logic: filter model.fields for f.nested_schema_name.is_some() — these are refs that weren't inlined even with expand=true
- JSON mode in render/json.rs already includes drill_deeper always — verify it handles this correctly
close_reason: Closed

title: Resolve inline list/pagination wrapper response schemas
description:
## Problem
When an API wraps list responses in an inline pagination object (e.g., `{data: T[], has_more: boolean, url: string}`), phyllotaxis shows no response schema information at all. This affects 129 of 587 Stripe endpoints (22%).

### Example: GET /v1/customers
The spec defines the 200 response with an inline schema containing:
- data: array of $ref customer
- has_more: boolean
- object: string (enum: "list")
- url: string (pattern: ^/v1/customers, maxLength: 5000)

Phyllotaxis shows:
```
Responses:
  200 Successful response.
```
JSON: `"schema_ref": null`

### Root Cause
The response schema resolution likely only handles direct `$ref` references. When the response schema is an inline object (even if it contains `$ref`s in its properties), it falls through and shows nothing.

## Key Files
- `src/commands/resources.rs` — endpoint detail, response rendering
- `src/models/resource.rs` — response model, check schema_ref extraction
- The response schema extraction probably does something like:
  ```
  if let ReferenceOr::Reference { reference } = schema {
      // extract ref name
  }
  // else: nothing (the bug)
  ```
  It needs to also handle `ReferenceOr::Item` for inline schemas.

## Expected Behavior
For paginated list endpoints, the output should show:
- Text: `200 Successful response. -> customer[] (paginated)` or similar
- JSON: `"schema_ref": "customer"` with additional pagination metadata, or an inline schema representation
- At minimum: show it's an inline object with a `data` array pointing to the item type

## Test Fixtures
- `tests/fixtures/stripe.yaml` — 129 affected endpoints
- `tests/fixtures/petstore.yaml` — has list endpoints (verify behavior)

## Success Criteria  
- [ ] `phyllotaxis --spec tests/fixtures/stripe.yaml resources customers GET /v1/customers` shows response schema info
- [ ] The item type within the data array is identified (e.g., "customer")
- [ ] Direct $ref responses still work correctly (no regression)
- [ ] JSON mode includes response schema information for inline schemas
- [ ] Existing tests pass: `cargo test`
close_reason: Fixed and merged to main

title: Fix array request body shown as Raw body (no schema)
description:
## Problem
Endpoints accepting array request bodies (e.g. POST /user/createWithList accepts User[]) display "Raw body (no schema)" instead of showing the array item schema fields.

## Root Cause
In src/commands/resources.rs, the request body extraction doesn't handle array-of-$ref schemas. The body fields come back empty, and src/render/text.rs line 241-242 shows "Raw body (no schema)" when body.fields.is_empty().

The fix needs to happen at the extraction level in resources.rs where request body schemas are parsed. When the schema is type:array with items.$ref, it should:
1. Set schema_ref to "TypeName[]" on the RequestBody model
2. Resolve the item schema's fields so they appear in the output

## Files to Change
- src/commands/resources.rs: Request body extraction logic (search for where RequestBody is built)
- src/render/text.rs:241-242: Optionally show array item type when fields are empty but schema_ref exists

## Success Criteria  
- `phyllotaxis resources user POST /user/createWithList --spec /tmp/petstore.json` shows User fields (id, username, etc.)
- The output indicates it's an array body, e.g. "Request Body (application/json) - User[]:"
- cargo test passes
close_reason: All fixes verified against Petstore and GitHub API specs

title: Task 10: Extract links from responses
description: In get_endpoint_detail, extract links from responses into ResponseLink vec. Note: link.operation is LinkOperation enum (OperationId/OperationRef), not link.operation_id. Plan: Task 10 (lines 673-806).
close_reason: Added extract_links_from_response and build_link_drill_command helpers. Updated extract_responses to accept api ref and populate links. Aggregated response links into Endpoint.links. 2 tests pass.

title: Add HEAD/OPTIONS/TRACE to build_link_drill_command
description:
## Problem
build_link_drill_command in resources.rs (lines 710-716) only checks 5 of 8 HTTP methods when resolving link operation IDs:
```rust
let methods: &[(&str, &Option<openapiv3::Operation>)] = &[
    ("GET", &path_item.get),
    ("POST", &path_item.post),
    ("PUT", &path_item.put),
    ("DELETE", &path_item.delete),
    ("PATCH", &path_item.patch),
];
```

Missing: HEAD, OPTIONS, TRACE. If a link points to one of these operations, the drill command will be None. Other places in the codebase (e.g., extract_resource_groups) correctly list all 8 methods.

## Fix
Add the 3 missing methods:
```rust
("HEAD", &path_item.head),
("OPTIONS", &path_item.options),
("TRACE", &path_item.trace),
```

## Success Criteria
- All 8 HTTP methods are checked in link resolution
- Existing tests pass
- Verify no other locations have the same gap (search for method lists)
close_reason: Closed

title: Add endpoint counts to resource list text output
description:
## Problem
The text-mode resource list shows only name + description. The JSON output already includes endpoint_count per resource but the text doesn't surface it. For large APIs (76 resources in Stripe, 45 in GitHub), knowing "customers (46)" vs "account_links (1)" is key orientation data.

## Root Cause
In src/render/text.rs:1068-1075 (render_resource_list), the format string only includes slug, marker, and description:
```rust
writeln!(out, "  {:<width$}  {}  {}",
    sanitize(&group.slug),
    marker,
    desc,
    width = max_slug,
).unwrap();
```

The ResourceGroup model already has an endpoints field (Vec<EndpointSummary>) whose len() gives the count.

## Fix
Add endpoint count in parentheses after the slug. Update the format string:
```rust
writeln!(out, "  {:<width$}  ({:>3})  {}  {}",
    sanitize(&group.slug),
    group.endpoints.len(),
    marker,
    desc,
    width = max_slug,
).unwrap();
```

## Files to Change
- src/render/text.rs:1068-1075 (render_resource_list - add count column)

## Success Criteria
- `phyllotaxis resources --spec /tmp/stripe-api.json` shows counts like "customers  ( 46)  ..."
- `phyllotaxis resources --spec /tmp/petstore.json` shows counts like "pet  ( 8)  ..."  
- Alignment is clean — counts are right-aligned in a fixed-width column
- cargo test passes
close_reason: All fixes verified against Petstore and GitHub API specs

title: Task 10: Callback count in overview
close_reason: Callback count added to overview: struct field, build() populates from list_all_callbacks, text renderer shows count, JSON renderer includes field, all 5 unit tests updated

Found during dogfooding against Petstore spec.

title: Fix schema name using user input casing instead of canonical spec name
description:
## Problem
Running "phyllotaxis schemas pet" shows "Schema: pet" instead of "Schema: Pet". The display should use the canonical schema name from the spec regardless of user input casing.

## Root Cause
In src/commands/schemas.rs:165, build_schema_model uses name.to_string() directly from user input:
```rust
Some(SchemaModel {
    name: name.to_string(),  // User's input, not canonical name
    ...
})
```

The find_schema function (line 20-52) does case-insensitive matching but only returns the Schema, not the matched key name.

## Fix
Option A: Change find_schema to return (key_name, &Schema) tuple so build_schema_model can use the canonical key.
Option B: Add a separate find_canonical_name function that returns the matching key from components.schemas.

Either way, line 165 should become: name: canonical_name.to_string()

## Files to Change
- src/commands/schemas.rs:20-52 (find_schema - return canonical name)
- src/commands/schemas.rs:165 (build_schema_model - use canonical name)

## Success Criteria
- `phyllotaxis schemas pet --spec /tmp/petstore.json` shows "Schema: Pet" (capital P)
- `phyllotaxis schemas PET --spec /tmp/petstore.json` also shows "Schema: Pet"
- Exact-case input still works: `phyllotaxis schemas Pet` shows "Schema: Pet"
- cargo test passes
close_reason: All fixes verified against Petstore and GitHub API specs

title: Fix default responses silently dropped from endpoint detail
description:
## Problem
OpenAPI specs can define a "default" response (catch-all for unexpected errors). Phyllotaxis silently ignores it. Many Petstore endpoints define default: {description: "Unexpected error"} which never appears in output.

## Root Cause
In src/commands/resources.rs:598-666 (extract_responses), the function only iterates operation.responses.responses (the StatusCode-keyed map). The default response is stored separately in operation.responses.default and is never accessed.

## Fix
After the main loop, check if operation.responses.default exists and add it as a Response with status_code "default":
```rust
if let Some(ref default_ref) = operation.responses.default {
    if let openapiv3::ReferenceOr::Item(resp) = default_ref {
        // Build Response with status_code: "default", same logic as the loop body
        responses.push(Response { status_code: "default".to_string(), ... });
    }
}
```

In text rendering (src/render/text.rs:257-267), the default response will fall into the "errors" bucket since its status_code doesn't start with '2'. Display it as "Default: description".

## Files to Change
- src/commands/resources.rs:598-666 (extract_responses - add default response handling after main loop)
- src/render/text.rs:257-267 (optionally special-case "default" status display)

## Success Criteria
- Petstore endpoints with default responses show them in output (e.g. "Default  Unexpected error")
- JSON output includes default response in responses array
- Default response appears in Errors section, not Responses section
- cargo test passes
close_reason: All fixes verified against Petstore and GitHub API specs

title: Task 3: Search results show match reason
close_reason: Implemented: match reason annotation on search results

title: Fix unbounded file reads in auto_detect_spec
description:
## Problem
`auto_detect_spec()` in `spec.rs:244` and `find_spec_candidates()` in `init.rs:156` read entire files into memory just to check the first 200 bytes for an OpenAPI header. If a working directory contains a multi-GB JSON or YAML file (common in data engineering), this causes OOM.

## Fix
Replace `std::fs::read_to_string(&path)` with bounded reads:

```rust
use std::io::Read;
let mut file = std::fs::File::open(&path)?;
let mut buf = vec![0u8; 200];
let n = file.read(&mut buf)?;
let peek = String::from_utf8_lossy(&buf[..n]);
```

Apply to both locations:
- `src/spec.rs:244` — `auto_detect_spec()`
- `src/commands/init.rs:156` — `find_spec_candidates()`

## Success Criteria
- Neither function reads more than 200 bytes when checking file headers
- Existing tests pass: `cargo test --locked`
- New unit test: mock a file larger than 200 bytes, verify only header is checked
close_reason: Closed

Found on GitHub API's webhook-config-insecure-ssl schema. Need to handle inline type variants, not just $ref-based ones.

title: Add --expand support for endpoint detail views
description: Currently --expand only affects schema detail views. On endpoint detail, the flag is accepted but does nothing — response/request schemas still show as references (e.g., '-> Pet'). For LLMs, expanding response schemas inline would save a tool call round-trip. All 3 dogfooding testers flagged this independently.
close_reason: Closed

title: Handle array-items-anyOf schema pattern (e.g. Slack objs_user)
description:
## Problem
Schemas defined as `items: { anyOf: [...] }` (an array whose items are an anyOf composition) render as completely empty — no fields, no composition info, nothing.

### Example: Slack `objs_user`
The schema is defined in tests/fixtures/slack.yaml as:
```yaml
objs_user:
  items:
    anyOf:
      - additionalProperties: false
        description: user object for non enterprise type
        properties:
          color:
            pattern: ^[a-fA-F0-9]{6}$
            type: string
          deleted:
            type: boolean
          enterprise_user:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/objs_enterprise_user"
          has_2fa:
            type: boolean
          id:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/defs_user_id"
          is_admin:
            type: boolean
          is_app_user:
            type: boolean
          # ... 30+ fields total across variants
```

Phyllotaxis output:
```
Schema: objs_user
```
JSON: `{"fields":[], "composition":null, "description":null}`

### Why existing fixes don't cover this
The anyOf/oneOf fix (commit 583c48d, bead phyllotaxis-9lk) handles two patterns:
1. **Top-level anyOf/oneOf** — `SchemaKind::AnyOf { any_of }` / `SchemaKind::OneOf { one_of }`
2. **Response-level anyOf/oneOf** — in `resolve_response_schema_ref()`

But `objs_user` is a third pattern: `SchemaKind::Type(Type::Array(ArrayType))` where the *items* field contains an anyOf. The schema kind is Array, not AnyOf, so the composition handling never triggers.

Also affected: `objs_conversation` in the Slack spec uses the same pattern.

## Key Files
- `src/commands/schemas.rs` — `build_schema_model()` is where schema detail is built. The Array type arm extracts item type but doesn't descend into items.anyOf.
- `src/commands/resources.rs` — `resolve_response_schema_ref()` handles response schemas (may also need this pattern)

## Expected Behavior
For `schemas objs_user`:
- Text mode should show fields from the anyOf variants (merged, or listed per variant)
- JSON mode should populate `fields` and/or `composition` 
- The composition field should indicate it's an "array of anyOf" pattern
- `--expand` should inline the variant schemas

## Test Fixtures
- `tests/fixtures/slack.yaml` — `objs_user` and `objs_conversation` with `items.anyOf` pattern
- `tests/fixtures/kitchen-sink.yaml` — add a test case for this pattern
- `tests/fixtures/petstore.yaml` — verify no regression

## Implementation Hint
In `build_schema_model()`, when handling `SchemaKind::Type(Type::Array(arr))`, check if `arr.items` resolves to a schema with `SchemaKind::AnyOf` or `SchemaKind::OneOf`. If so, use the existing `extract_composition_fields()` function to get the merged fields from the variants.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `phyllotaxis --spec tests/fixtures/slack.yaml schemas objs_user` shows fields (not empty)
- [ ] `phyllotaxis --spec tests/fixtures/slack.yaml --json schemas objs_user` has non-empty fields array
- [ ] JSON output indicates the array-of-anyOf composition
- [ ] `objs_conversation` also shows fields
- [ ] Existing tests pass: `cargo test`
- [ ] No regression on array schemas that already work
close_reason: Fixed array-items-anyOf pattern. Added extract_items_composition() helper that handles both AnySchema (implicit array) and Type::Array paths. 30 fields now render for objs_user, objs_conversation also works.

title: Remote URL loading for OpenAPI documents
description:
Support loading OpenAPI documents from HTTP/HTTPS URLs in addition to local file paths. This removes the 'download first' friction, especially useful for LLM agents that know a spec URL but don't have the file locally.

Example: phyll --doc https://petstore3.swagger.io/api/v3/openapi.json --resources

Should include local caching to avoid re-downloading on every call. Cache key could be URL + ETag/Last-Modified, stored in ~/.cache/phyllotaxis/ or similar.

Needs a lightweight HTTP client dependency (ureq or reqwest with minimal features).

Inspired by: kadykov/mcp-openapi-schema-explorer and SagenKoder/go-openapi-exploration-mcp-server both support this.

title: Error when method provided without path in endpoint lookup
description: Running 'resources pet GET' (method without path) silently falls through to showing the resource listing instead of erroring. For LLMs this is mode confusion — they expect endpoint detail but get a resource listing and won't know the command was wrong. Should error with 'Missing PATH argument' or similar.
close_reason: Closed

title: Replace expect() with proper error handling on user-facing paths
description:
## Problem
Several .expect() calls panic on user-facing code paths instead of returning clean errors:

### main.rs (crash risk)
- Line 134: `std::env::current_dir().expect("cannot determine current directory")` — panics if cwd is deleted
- Line 161: `.to_str().expect("spec path not valid UTF-8")` — panics on non-UTF-8 Linux paths

### init.rs (crash risk on write failures)
- Line 189: `write_init_config(...).expect("failed to write .phyllotaxis.yaml")` — panics on read-only fs
- Line 223: `.expect("failed to read input")` — panics on stdin failure
- Line 243: `write_init_config(...).expect("failed to write .phyllotaxis.yaml")`
- Line 256: `.expect("failed to read input")`
- Line 281: `.expect("failed to read input")`

NOTE: Lines 331, 378, 385, 408, 414 in init.rs are in #[cfg(test)] blocks — those .expect() calls are fine.

## Fix
Replace with anyhow error handling:
```rust
// main.rs:134
let cwd = std::env::current_dir()
    .context("Cannot determine current directory")?;

// main.rs:161
.map(|p| p.to_string_lossy().to_string());

// init.rs production paths
write_init_config(&config_path, &stored)
    .context("Failed to write .phyllotaxis.yaml")?;
```

## Success Criteria
- Zero .expect() calls in production code paths (test code is fine)
- All error paths produce clean user-facing messages via anyhow
- All existing tests pass
close_reason: Closed

title: Integration tests: multi-file happy path (4 tests)
description:
Add run_with_multi_file helper. Tests: overview text, overview JSON, schemas list, resources list — all against multi-file fixture. See implementation plan Task 6.

IMPORTANT CONTEXT: The run_with_multi_file helper uses a local variable for &spec that must stay alive for the duration of the vec! macro. The plan's code captures spec as a local String then takes &spec — this is correct and will not have lifetime issues. The helper pattern matches run_with_petstore and run_with_kitchen_sink exactly.

SUCCESS CRITERIA: cargo test --locked test_multi_file passes all 4 tests. All tests assert exit code 0, title 'Multi-File API', Pet and Error schemas present, and pets/Pets resource present.
close_reason: Closed

title: Resolve inline request body schemas (non-$ref bodies silently dropped)
description:
## Problem
When an endpoint defines its request body with an inline schema (not using $ref), phyllotaxis shows "Raw body (no schema)" — silently dropping all parameter information. This makes the tool useless for write operations on specs that use inline schemas.

### Example: GitLab POST /broadcast_messages
The spec (tests/fixtures/gitlab.yaml, lines ~2113-2161) defines 8 properties inline:
- message (required, string)
- starts_at (string, datetime)
- ends_at (string, datetime)  
- color (string)
- font (string)
- target_access_levels (array of integers)
- target_path (string)
- broadcast_type (string)
- dismissable (boolean)

Phyllotaxis shows:
```
Request Body (application/json): Raw body (no schema)
```

### Also affected: GitLab POST /admin/clusters/add (10+ properties)

This was previously reported as "$ref request bodies not resolved" but the problem is broader — it also affects non-$ref inline schemas.

## Key Files
- `src/commands/resources.rs` — endpoint detail rendering, look for request body extraction logic
- `src/models/resource.rs` — resource/endpoint model, check how request bodies are parsed
- `src/spec.rs` — spec loading, may have request body resolution logic

## Investigation Steps
1. Find where request body content is extracted from the OpenAPI struct
2. Check if it only handles `ReferenceOr::Reference` and ignores `ReferenceOr::Item` for request bodies
3. Also check if it only handles `ReferenceOr::Reference` for the schema within `MediaType`
4. The fix should handle both $ref'd and inline schemas

## Test Fixtures
- `tests/fixtures/gitlab.yaml` — has inline request body schemas
- `tests/fixtures/stripe.yaml` — has $ref'd request body schemas (verify no regression)
- `tests/fixtures/petstore.yaml` — has request bodies (verify no regression)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `phyllotaxis --spec tests/fixtures/gitlab.yaml resources broadcast_messages POST /broadcast_messages` shows all 8 request body fields
- [ ] `phyllotaxis --spec tests/fixtures/gitlab.yaml resources admin POST /admin/clusters/add` shows request body fields
- [ ] JSON mode includes request body fields with types, required flags, descriptions
- [ ] Existing $ref-based request bodies still work (test with petstore.yaml and stripe.yaml)
- [ ] Existing tests pass: `cargo test`
close_reason: Fixed and merged to main

title: Reject empty search term instead of returning everything
description:
## Problem
Running `phyllotaxis search ""` returns all resources, endpoints, and schemas with no warning. On the GitHub spec this dumps 3,123 lines. Should either reject empty queries or warn the results are unfiltered.

## Root Cause
In src/commands/search.rs:43-44, the search function accepts any term including empty string. An empty term.to_lowercase() matches contains() on everything.

## Fix
At the start of the search function (or in main.rs where search is dispatched), check if the term is empty/whitespace and return an error:
```rust
if term.trim().is_empty() {
    eprintln!("Error: Please provide a search term.");
    std::process::exit(1);
}
```

Or handle in main.rs before calling search().

## Files to Change
- src/main.rs (where search subcommand is dispatched) OR src/commands/search.rs:43

## Success Criteria
- `phyllotaxis search "" --spec /tmp/petstore.json` shows an error message, doesn't dump all results
- `phyllotaxis search "  " --spec /tmp/petstore.json` same behavior (whitespace-only)
- Normal searches still work
- cargo test passes
close_reason: All fixes verified against Petstore and GitHub API specs

title: Replace process::exit(1) with anyhow::bail!() throughout main.rs
description:
## Problem
`main.rs` has 6 instances of `eprintln!() + std::process::exit(1)` (lines 216, 304, 347, 384, 423, 501) and `init.rs` has 1 (line 184). These bypass RAII destructors and create a dual error handling path. The comment says "BUG-3 fix" but the real fix is bail!().

A prior worktree branch (agent-a1de75c5) implemented the bail!() approach correctly. That code was not merged — the current code uses the process::exit workaround instead.

## Fix
Replace all 7 instances of:
```rust
eprintln!("Error: {message}");
std::process::exit(1);
```
with:
```rust
anyhow::bail!("{message}");
```

For JSON mode errors, embed the JSON error in the bail message or use a wrapper type.

For "Did you mean?" suggestions, build the full message before calling bail!():
```rust
let mut msg = format!("Resource '{}' not found", name);
if let Some(suggestions) = suggest_similar(...) {
    msg.push_str(&format!("\n\nDid you mean?\n{}", suggestions));
}
anyhow::bail!("{msg}");
```

## Locations
- `src/main.rs:216` — resource not found
- `src/main.rs:304` — resource not found (JSON)
- `src/main.rs:347` — endpoint not found
- `src/main.rs:384` — schema not found (JSON)
- `src/main.rs:423` — schema not found
- `src/main.rs:501` — callback not found
- `src/commands/init.rs:184` — config already exists

## Success Criteria
- Zero instances of `process::exit(1)` in production code
- All errors flow through the single handler in main()
- "Did you mean?" suggestions still appear
- JSON error output still works
- All existing tests pass
close_reason: Closed

title: Add fuzzy matching fallback to search on zero results
description: Search uses substring matching (.contains()) which is correct for the main path. But when search returns zero results, it should suggest similar terms using the existing Jaro-Winkler infrastructure (already in resources.rs, schemas.rs, callbacks.rs). This prevents LLMs from concluding an API doesn't have a feature just because of a misspelling. Only activate on zero results to avoid noise.

title: Resolve anyOf/oneOf schema compositions that render as completely empty
description:
## Problem
Schemas that use `anyOf` or `oneOf` composition patterns render as completely empty — no fields, no title, no description. This affects both text and JSON output, even with --expand.

### Affected patterns:

**Pattern 1: Schema wrapping anyOf (Slack)**
Slack's `objs_user` and `objs_conversation` schemas use `items: { anyOf: [...] }` wrapping multiple object variants. Result:
```
Schema: objs_user
```
JSON: `{"fields":[], "composition":null, "description":null}`

The actual schema has 30+ fields (id, name, real_name, is_admin, is_bot, profile, etc.) that are completely invisible.

**Pattern 2: Response anyOf composition (Stripe)**
13 Stripe endpoints return `anyOf` in response schemas (e.g., `GET /v1/customers/{customer}` returns `anyOf [customer, deleted_customer]`). Phyllotaxis shows `schema_ref: null` for these.

Compare: `POST /v1/customers` uses a direct `$ref` and correctly shows `-> customer`.

## Key Files
- `src/models/schema.rs` — schema model extraction, likely where anyOf/oneOf needs handling
- `src/render/text.rs` and `src/render/json.rs` — rendering (may need to handle composition display)
- `src/commands/schemas.rs` — schema command
- `src/commands/resources.rs` — endpoint detail (response schema resolution)

## Test Fixtures
- `tests/fixtures/slack.yaml` — has `objs_user` with `items.anyOf` pattern
- `tests/fixtures/stripe.yaml` — has `anyOf` response schemas on GET endpoints
- `tests/fixtures/kitchen-sink.yaml` — may have `oneOf` patterns already (check existing tests)

## Expected Behavior
1. Schema detail for `objs_user` should show the fields from all anyOf variants (or list the variants with their fields)
2. Endpoint responses with anyOf should display all possible return types: `-> customer | deleted_customer`
3. JSON output should populate the `composition` field and/or `fields` array appropriately
4. --expand should inline the variant schemas

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `phyllotaxis --spec tests/fixtures/slack.yaml schemas objs_user` shows fields (not empty)
- [ ] `phyllotaxis --spec tests/fixtures/stripe.yaml resources customers GET /v1/customers/{customer}` shows response schema ref(s)
- [ ] JSON mode includes anyOf/oneOf variant information
- [ ] --expand inlines variant schemas
- [ ] Existing tests pass: `cargo test`
- [ ] No regression on schemas that already work (test with petstore.yaml)
close_reason: Fixed and merged to main

close_reason: Implemented: suppress empty param sections + dynamic flag column alignment

title: Fix duplicate error messages printing twice on every error path
description:
## Problem
Every error message in phyllotaxis prints twice. This affects ALL error paths: nonexistent resources, misspelled names, empty searches, unknown subcommands, wrong HTTP methods, missing path arguments. Confirmed across Slack, GitLab, and Stripe specs.

Example:
```
$ phyllotaxis resources nonexistent
Error: Resource 'nonexistent' not found.

Error: Resource 'nonexistent' not found.
```

Also duplicates in --json mode and even clap's own unrecognized subcommand errors.

## Root Cause Investigation
The error is likely being printed once by the command handler (via eprintln! or anyhow) and once by the top-level error handler in main.rs. Check:
- `src/main.rs` — look for how errors from commands are propagated and displayed
- Each command in `src/commands/` — look for explicit eprintln! of errors before returning Err()
- The pattern is probably: command prints error AND returns Err, then main catches Err and prints again

## Fix
Ensure errors are printed exactly once. The cleanest approach:
- Commands should return Err(anyhow!(...)) WITHOUT printing
- main.rs should have ONE place that catches errors and prints them
- OR commands print and return Ok(()) for handled errors, only returning Err for unexpected failures

## Test Fixtures
- `tests/fixtures/petstore.yaml` (small, fast for testing)
- `tests/fixtures/slack.yaml` (larger)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Running any error-producing command prints the error exactly once
- [ ] Applies to: resource not found, schema not found, endpoint not found, empty search, missing args
- [ ] Both text and --json modes print errors once
- [ ] Existing tests pass: `cargo test`
- [ ] Manual verification with at least 3 different error types
close_reason: Fixed and merged to main

title: Binary name evaluation: phyll vs phyllo vs phyl
description: Feedback from OpenAPI community reviewer: kept wanting to type 'phyllo' instead of 'phyll'. Double-L without a vowel after feels unnatural. Consider phyl (single L) or phyllo (complete prefix). This is a branding/UX decision. Consider: cargo install name, brew tap name, muscle memory, discoverability.

title: Trim double blank lines when description is empty
description:
K8s spec has no info.description, leaving double blank line between title and Top Resources in overview. Similar gaps in endpoint detail when summary is absent.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- No double blank lines in overview when description is absent
- No double blank lines in endpoint detail when summary is absent

- Simple conditional checks, minimal change

title: Add spec file size limit before loading
description:
## Problem
`spec.rs:196` calls `std::fs::read_to_string(&spec_path)` with no file size check. A malicious or accidentally huge spec file can exhaust memory. In LLM-assisted workflows where spec paths come from automation, this is a real risk.

## Fix
Add a size check before loading:

```rust
let metadata = std::fs::metadata(&spec_path)
    .with_context(|| format!("Failed to stat {}", spec_path.display()))?;
const MAX_SPEC_SIZE: u64 = 100 * 1024 * 1024; // 100MB
if metadata.len() > MAX_SPEC_SIZE {
    anyhow::bail!("Spec file {} is too large ({} bytes, max {} bytes)",
        spec_path.display(), metadata.len(), MAX_SPEC_SIZE);
}
```

## Success Criteria
- Files over 100MB are rejected with a clear error message
- Existing tests pass
- New test: verify the error message for oversized files (use a temp file)
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement bundle_refs with circular ref detection + 2 unit tests

IMPORTANT CONTEXT: Tests go in tests/lib_tests.rs (not src/spec.rs). lib_tests.rs currently has no tempfile usage — the circular ref test must add its own fs::write calls without a pre-existing tempfile import pattern to copy from. The file does import std::path::PathBuf already.

SUCCESS CRITERIA: cargo test --locked bundle_refs passes both tests. phyllotaxis::spec::bundle_refs is pub and accessible from lib_tests.rs. All pre-existing tests still pass (bundle_refs not yet called from load_spec).

title: Include 'Did you mean?' suggestions in JSON error output
description:
## Problem
When a user misspells a resource or schema name, text mode shows helpful "Did you mean?" suggestions, but JSON mode strips them entirely. Since --json is the LLM-primary output mode, this means LLMs cannot self-correct from typos.

### Text mode (correct):
```
Error: Resource 'paymnet_intents' not found.
Did you mean:
  phyllotaxis resources payment_intents
  phyllotaxis resources payment_links
```

### JSON mode (missing suggestions):
```json
{"error":"Resource 'paymnet_intents' not found."}
```

### Expected JSON output:
```json
{
  "error": "Resource 'paymnet_intents' not found.",
  "suggestions": [
    "phyllotaxis resources payment_intents",
    "phyllotaxis resources payment_links"
  ]
}
```

## Key Files
- `src/render/json.rs` — JSON error rendering, likely where suggestions need to be added
- `src/render/text.rs` — text error rendering (has suggestions, use as reference)
- `src/commands/resources.rs` — resource not found error path
- `src/commands/schemas.rs` — schema not found error path

## Investigation
1. Find where "Did you mean?" suggestions are generated (likely a fuzzy matching function)
2. Find where errors are serialized to JSON
3. The suggestions are generated but not passed to the JSON renderer
4. Need to include them in the JSON error object

## Test Fixtures
- `tests/fixtures/stripe.yaml` — large spec, good for testing fuzzy matches
- `tests/fixtures/petstore.yaml` — small spec for quick iteration

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `phyllotaxis --spec tests/fixtures/stripe.yaml --json resources paymnet_intents` returns JSON with a `suggestions` array
- [ ] `phyllotaxis --spec tests/fixtures/stripe.yaml --json schemas custmer` returns JSON with suggestions
- [ ] Suggestions match what text mode shows
- [ ] Empty suggestions array (not null) when no close matches exist
- [ ] Existing tests pass: `cargo test`
close_reason: Fixed and merged to main

title: Make --expand inline nested objects in request bodies
description:
## Problem
The --expand flag works correctly for response schemas (inlining nested schema fields) but does NOT expand nested objects in request bodies. For endpoints with deeply nested request parameters, an LLM cannot see the full structure.

### Example: Stripe POST /v1/subscriptions
```
$ phyllotaxis --spec tests/fixtures/stripe.yaml resources subscriptions POST /v1/subscriptions --expand
...
Request Body (application/x-www-form-urlencoded):
  automatic_tax     object  (optional)  Automatic tax settings...
  billing_mode      object  (optional)  Controls how prorations...
```

JSON with --expand:
```json
{"name": "automatic_tax", "type_display": "object", "nested_schema_name": null, "nested_fields": []}
```

The response section IS correctly expanded — the full subscription schema with nested types is inlined. The inconsistency is only on the request body side.

## Key Files
- `src/commands/resources.rs` — look for how --expand is applied to response vs request body
- `src/models/resource.rs` — request body field model, check if it supports nested_fields population
- `src/render/text.rs` and `src/render/json.rs` — rendering of request body fields

## Investigation
1. Find where --expand triggers response schema expansion
2. Find the equivalent code path for request body fields
3. The request body fields likely come from a different extraction path that doesn't apply the same recursive expansion
4. Need to apply similar expansion logic to request body object-typed fields

## Test Fixtures
- `tests/fixtures/stripe.yaml` — has deeply nested request body objects (subscriptions, payment_intents)
- `tests/fixtures/petstore.yaml` — simpler request bodies (verify no regression)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `phyllotaxis --spec tests/fixtures/stripe.yaml resources subscriptions POST /v1/subscriptions --expand` shows nested fields inside `automatic_tax` and other object params
- [ ] JSON mode with --expand populates `nested_fields` for request body objects
- [ ] Response-side expansion continues to work (no regression)
- [ ] Non-expanded mode is unchanged
- [ ] Existing tests pass: `cargo test`
close_reason: Fixed and merged to main

title: Modify load_spec for two-pass loading + 1 unit test
description:
Replace direct YAML->openapiv3 parse with Value->bundle_refs->from_value two-pass flow. Test: load multi-file fixture and assert schemas/paths are bundled. See implementation plan Task 5.

IMPORTANT CONTEXT: The plan says to replace lines 212-214 in spec.rs with the two-pass code. Actual line numbers may shift as Tasks 2/3/4 add new functions before the cfg(test) block — agent must locate the block by content, not line number. The test calls load_spec(Some(&spec_path), &manifest_dir) where manifest_dir is a PathBuf; &PathBuf coerces to &Path, so the call is correct. The or_else chain for YAML->JSON parsing must include a type annotation: serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&content) to resolve type inference.

SUCCESS CRITERIA: cargo test --locked test_load_spec_multi_file passes. All 263 pre-existing tests (191 integration + 66 unit + others) continue to pass. The old single-pass parse block is fully replaced — no dead code.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix resource_slug empty in search JSON output
description:
## Problem
When running search with --json, endpoint results have resource_slug: "" despite the field existing on the EndpointMatch struct (src/commands/search.rs:31).

## Root Cause
In src/commands/search.rs, when building EndpointMatch results, the resource_slug field is likely set to an empty string because the search iterates paths/operations directly rather than through the resource group extraction.

## Fix
When building endpoint matches in the search function, derive the resource_slug from the operation's first tag (same logic used by extract_resource_groups to build slugs). The slug is: first tag, lowercased, with spaces/slashes replaced by underscores.

## Files to Change
- src/commands/search.rs:43+ (search function - populate resource_slug from operation tags)

## Success Criteria
- `phyllotaxis search "subscription" --json --spec /tmp/stripe-api.json` shows non-empty resource_slug on endpoint matches
- resource_slug matches the slug shown by `phyllotaxis resources`
- cargo test passes
close_reason: All fixes verified against Petstore and GitHub API specs

title: CLI restructure: subcommands to flags-based interface
description: Feedback from OpenAPI community reviewer: Current subcommand model (phyll resources, phyll schemas, etc.) locks users into examining one thing at a time. The verb is always 'examine' so subcommands are nouns that should be flags. Proposed: phyll <document> --show resources,schemas or phyll <document> --resources pets --schemas. Document as positional arg keeps it near cursor for easy terminal editing. Reference: clig.dev. Also consider Redocly CLI patterns. This is a fundamental interface redesign.

title: Fix default response classified as error

title: Add OAuth2 flow details: scopes, authorization URL, flow type
description:
## Problem
The auth command shows OAuth2 schemes with minimal info: just "oauth2" as the detail string. Missing: authorization URL, token URL, flow type (implicit/authorizationCode/clientCredentials), and available scopes. Per-endpoint scope requirements are also missing.

## Root Cause
In src/commands/auth.rs:52-53, the OAuth2 match arm discards the flows:
```rust
openapiv3::SecurityScheme::OAuth2 { description, .. } => {
    ("oauth2".to_string(), "oauth2".to_string(), description.clone())
}
```

The openapiv3::SecurityScheme::OAuth2 variant has a flows field containing OAuth2Flows with implicit_flow, authorization_code_flow, client_credentials_flow, password_flow — each containing authorization_url, token_url, and scopes.

## Fix
1. In auth.rs, extract the OAuth2 flows and build a richer detail string or add new fields to SecuritySchemeInfo for flows and scopes.
2. Add scopes list to the auth model.
3. In text rendering, display flow type, URLs, and scopes.
4. (Stretch) In endpoint detail, show required scopes per-endpoint from the security requirement objects.

## Files to Change
- src/commands/auth.rs:52-53 (extract OAuth2 flows, scopes, URLs)
- src/models/ (may need new fields on SecuritySchemeInfo for flows/scopes)
- src/render/text.rs (render OAuth2 details in auth view)
- src/render/json.rs (include OAuth2 details in JSON output)

## Success Criteria
- `phyllotaxis auth --spec /tmp/petstore.json` shows petstore_auth with:
  - Flow type: implicit
  - Authorization URL: https://petstore3.swagger.io/oauth/authorize
  - Scopes: write:pets, read:pets (with descriptions)
- JSON output includes flows and scopes
- cargo test passes
close_reason: All fixes verified against Petstore and GitHub API specs

title: Terminology: rename 'spec' to 'document' throughout codebase
description: Feedback from OpenAPI community reviewer: 'spec' is colloquially used but incorrect. The OpenAPI Specification is the standard itself (OAS 3.0, 3.1). The YAML/JSON files are 'OpenAPI documents' (OADs). Rename --spec flag, config keys, help text, error messages, and docs to use correct terminology. Marketing/SEO can still mention 'spec' for discoverability but the tool itself should use proper terms.
close_reason: Closed

title: Multi-flag JSON produces single document
description:
When using --json --resources --auth, each handler does its own println(), producing NDJSON (separate JSON objects per line) instead of a single valid JSON document. Breaks JSON.parse() on full output.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- phyll --json --resources --auth produces a single valid JSON document with top-level keys
- echo output | jq . succeeds for multi-flag JSON
- Single-flag JSON mode unchanged (same shape as before)
- Text mode multi-flag unchanged (concatenated output)
- Test: serde_json::from_str(&stdout) succeeds for multi-flag JSON

IMPLEMENTATION:
- File: src/main.rs, run() function (lines 235-284)
- Approach: In JSON mode, have handlers return String instead of printing. Collect outputs as (key, serde_json::Value) pairs. Merge into single JSON object. Print once.
- Change handler signatures or add a collector to Ctx
- Text mode: keep current println!() behavior (concatenation is natural for text)
- Keys: resources, schemas, auth, callbacks, endpoint (match flag names)
- This is the largest change of the batch — affects all 5 handler functions
close_reason: Closed

title: --context should expand request body inline objects
description:
When --context is used with --endpoint, inline object properties in request bodies (like Stripe's address, invoice_settings) show as 'type: object' without field details. Fix: in handle_endpoint() at main.rs:496, pass ctx.expand || context instead of ctx.expand to get_endpoint_detail(). One-line change.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- phyll petstore.yaml --endpoint POST /pets --context shows expanded Owner fields without explicit --expand
- Existing --expand behavior unchanged
- Test: run_with_petstore(&["--endpoint", "POST", "/pets", "--context"]) shows 'Owner:' with nested fields

IMPLEMENTATION:
- File: src/main.rs, handle_endpoint() function (~line 500)
- Change: let expand = ctx.expand || context; then pass expand to get_endpoint_detail()
- Add integration test for --context implying --expand
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix duplicate error messages (errors print twice)
description:
Every error message prints twice to stderr. Confirmed by QA testing across multiple scenarios: missing document, invalid document, old --spec flag rejection. This was flagged in the original dogfooding report as a MEDIUM priority bug.

ROOT CAUSE (suspected):
The error handler in main() at src/main.rs prints the error, but something else (likely clap or a previous eprintln! call path) is also printing it. Need to trace all error output paths.

REPRODUCTION:
- Run: phyll (with no document in scope) — error prints twice
- Run: phyll --doc /dev/null — parse error prints twice

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- Every error scenario produces exactly ONE error message on stderr
- JSON mode errors produce exactly one JSON error object
- Exit codes unchanged
close_reason: Bug was already fixed in commit c1fe239. Verified: all error scenarios produce exactly one message on stderr. All 87 integration tests pass.

title: Use bin_name in JSON renderer drill-deeper hints
description:
## Problem
The JSON renderer in json.rs accepts a bin_name parameter but ignores it (prefixed with underscore: _bin_name) in most functions. Instead, drill-deeper hints are hardcoded to "phyll":
```rust
drill_deeper: "phyll resources <name>".to_string(),
```

If the binary is installed under a different name, JSON output will show wrong commands.

## Affected functions in src/render/json.rs
- render_resource_list (line 163) — _bin_name unused
- render_resource_detail (line 200) — _bin_name unused
- render_schema_list (line 257) — _bin_name unused
- render_schema_detail (line 276) — _bin_name unused
- render_endpoint_detail, render_search_results, render_auth, etc.

## Fix
Replace _bin_name with bin_name and use it in format strings:
```rust
drill_deeper: format!("{} resources <name>", bin_name),
```

## Success Criteria
- All drill-deeper hints in JSON output use the actual binary name
- No _bin_name parameters remain (all should be used)
- JSON integration tests updated to verify bin_name appears in output
close_reason: Closed

title: Hide resource groups with 0 endpoints

Twilio's spec defines tags that have no operations. extract_resource_groups() creates groups from global tags array, but some never get endpoints. Groups with (0) count are noise.

IMPLEMENTATION:
- File: src/commands/resources.rs, extract_resource_groups() (~line 95)
- Add filter before return: result.retain(|g| \!g.endpoints.is_empty())
- Place AFTER the has_endpoints check and path-prefix fallback (line ~97), not before
- Add test with kitchen-sink fixture verifying no empty groups
